entities.name: EDIBLE INSECTS
entities.description: Insects used for human and animal consumption in African cultures

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where edible insect consumption and processing are reviewed

entities.name: POSTHARVEST PROCESSES
entities.description: Activities and technologies applied to insects after harvest to ensure safety and quality

entities.name: NUTRITION
entities.description: The health and dietary implications of consuming edible insects

entities.name: SAFETY
entities.description: The prevention of hazards in the processing and consumption of edible insects

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITION SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring global access to nutritious food, which edible insects can support

entities.name: PROCESSING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technical methods used to transform raw insects into food and feed products

entities.name: PACKAGING
entities.description: Techniques used to enclose insect products to improve shelf-life

entities.name: STORAGE TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods used to preserve insect products over time

entities.name: SHELF-LIFE
entities.description: The duration for which insect products remain fit for consumption

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL VALUE
entities.description: The nutrient content and quality of edible insects

entities.name: FUNCTIONALITY
entities.description: The technical properties of insect products for use in food development

entities.name: TRADITIONAL KNOWLEDGE
entities.description: Indigenous practices and wisdom regarding the processing of insects

entities.name: QUALITY
entities.description: The overall standard and characteristics of the final insect product

entities.name: PRESENTATION
entities.description: The visual and physical appeal of insect-based products
